creator: rfg@tristatelogic.com
text:
Attempting to print even a relatively trivial web page (e.g. Google's home
page) from within Opera (12.14), either directly to a Postscript-capable
printer or else to a (Postscript) disk file results in a Postscript file
that, when rendered, looks exceptionally mangled.

The attached file is what Opera is currently producing in the way of .ps
output when asked to print Google's home page.  My Postscript-compatible
printer is able to print this file, however what comes out looks quite
mangled, and not at all like Google's actual home page.  (I would have
tried using ghostview also to perform a test-rendering on this .ps file,
but unfortunately, ghostview is currently coredumping on me... a separate
problem for which I have just filed a separate PR.)

Please note that in addition to Opera 12.14, I also currently have installed
on this same system firefox-18.0.1,1 and it appears to have no problems
generating Postscript that, when sent to my Postscript-compatible printer,
does in fact look rather a lot like Google's current home page.

Fix: I have no idea how to fix this.  I guess that Opera isn't open source, so
I guess the Opera maintainers will have to dig into this problem.


P.S.  Please note that this exact same problem also afflicted the Opera
version 12.13 port.

How-To-Repeat: 
portinstall www/opera
opera

{{Then browse to http://www.google.com/ and request opera to print the current
page to a .ps file, and then shove that file out to some Postscript-compatible
printer and then notice the difference between what is on the printed pages
(3) and what Google's actual current home page looks like.}}
